name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V. is a full-stack AI infrastructure company building large-scale GPU clusters, cloud platforms, and developer tools to service the global AI industry. Formerly Yandex N.V., the company rebranded in August 2024 after divesting its Russian operations. Portfolio includes TripleTen (edtech re-skilling), Avride (autonomous driving and delivery robotics), and a strategic alliance with NVIDIA. Founded in 1989, headquartered in Schiphol, Netherlands.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Nebius executed one of the most dramatic corporate transformations in recent history — divesting its entire Russian Yandex business and pivoting to become a pure-play AI infrastructure company in 2024, then growing revenue from $91.5M to $529.8M (479% YoY) in 2025 [1]. This is a transformative reinvention that redefined the company's identity, market, and customer base. AI Deployment (2/2): Nebius IS an AI infrastructure company — its core product is GPU clusters and AI cloud platforms shipping to customers globally, with core AI cloud revenue growing 830% YoY in Q4 2025 and an annualized revenue run rate of $1.25B exiting 2025 [1]. Market Share (1/2): Positioned as a leading 'neocloud' provider competing with CoreWeave and Lambda, but still small relative to hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP); rapidly gaining share in the independent AI cloud market through its NVIDIA strategic alliance [1][2]. Product Pipeline (2/2): Guided to $3-3.4B revenue in 2026 and $7-9B ARR by year-end 2026, with $16-20B capex planned for infrastructure buildout; specific data center expansions in Finland, France, and the US provide clear pipeline visibility [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending data not separately disclosed, though the company's entire business is essentially R&D-driven infrastructure development.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Nebius's purpose — building full-stack infrastructure to 'power the global AI revolution' — is clear and identifies the company's role, but is functional rather than deeply purposeful; it describes what the company does rather than a vision of a transformed future [2]. Community Impact (1/2): The company reports that 94% of electricity consumed comes from low-carbon sources (renewables and nuclear) and recycles nearly 100% of retired hardware, demonstrating environmental responsibility in a capital-intensive sector [3]. TripleTen edtech platform provides re-skilling for technology careers. Employee Retention (1/2): With only 1,543 employees, Nebius operates with a lean, mission-driven team; founder Arkady Volozh's return as CEO in 2024 provides leadership continuity, and the management team includes long-tenured executives from the Yandex era [2]. However, no specific retention or turnover data is available. Insider Buying (1/2): Volozh and management hold meaningful equity stakes from the Yandex restructuring, though recent insider transaction data shows primarily stock grants rather than open-market purchases.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Revenue growth is extraordinary — 684% YoY and ~240% 3-year CAGR from $13.5M (2022) to $529.8M (2025), both scoring at maximum [1]. Forward PEG of 0.63 is well below the 1.0 target, reflecting that growth expectations are achievable relative to valuation. D/E of 132.4% is elevated due to $9.6B in debt financing data center expansion, scoring 0.73. ROE of 14.1% is near the 15% target. TTM profit margin of 93.1% reflects the one-time gain from the Yandex Russia divestiture rather than operating profitability — operating margins are -32% as the company invests heavily ahead of revenue [1]. Forward P/E of 643x scores 0, reflecting pre-profit hypergrowth valuation. Free cash flow is deeply negative at -$6.1B due to $4.1B capex in 2025 with $16-20B planned for 2026 [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Nebius IS AI infrastructure — the company builds and operates the GPU clusters, cloud platforms, and developer tools that power AI workloads globally [1][2]. AI cannot disrupt a company whose entire business is enabling AI; increasing AI adoption directly increases demand for Nebius's services. The strategic alliance with NVIDIA positions Nebius as a preferred partner for next-generation GPU deployments. Physical data center infrastructure, complex supply chain management, and deep technical expertise in GPU cluster optimization create substantial barriers to entry. AI Opportunity (1/1): Nebius generates 100% of its revenue from AI-related infrastructure and services, with core AI cloud revenue growing 830% YoY in Q4 2025 and a $1.25B annualized run rate [1].

infinite_game.summary: Nebius demonstrates meaningful infinite game characteristics through founder Arkady Volozh's return and the company's willingness to completely reinvent itself from a Russian internet conglomerate into a global AI infrastructure player. The transformative pivot and long-term capex commitment ($16-20B in 2026) reflect conviction in a durable cause. However, the company is too young in its current form to have been tested through multiple cycles, and the aggressive capital deployment creates existential execution risk.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Nebius has a genuine cause — building the infrastructure layer that enables the global AI revolution — that is distinctive, forward-looking, and connects the company's work to a larger technological transformation [2]. The complete divestiture of the profitable Yandex Russia business to pursue this cause demonstrates that purpose shapes strategic decisions. However, the company is only two years into its reinvented identity; the cause has not yet been tested through market downturns or leadership transitions beyond the founding team.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): The decision to divest the entire Yandex Russia business — the company's core profit engine — and reinvent as an AI infrastructure company represents extraordinary strategic courage [2]. Committing $4.1B in capex in 2025 and $16-20B in 2026 against $530M of revenue demonstrates willingness to sacrifice near-term profitability for long-term capability. Volozh's return as CEO after stepping aside during geopolitical tensions shows personal commitment to the long-term vision [1].

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): At 1,543 employees, Nebius operates with a lean team where individual contributions are visible and valued [2]. The management team includes long-tenured executives from the Yandex era who chose to stay through the transformative pivot, suggesting organizational commitment. Chief People Officer Sarah Boulogne's C-suite presence signals cultural investment. However, the company is too young in its current form to have established deep organizational trust patterns, and rapid scaling from 1,543 employees to support $16-20B capex will test team cohesion.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): Nebius executed one of the most dramatic corporate reinventions in modern history — divesting its entire Russian internet business (Yandex search, ride-hailing, e-commerce, food delivery) to become a pure-play AI infrastructure company [2]. This is a textbook example of existential flexibility driven by purpose rather than desperation; the company chose to abandon its established business to pursue AI infrastructure before the market demanded it.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): Nebius positions itself competitively against hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, GCP) and neocloud peers (CoreWeave, Lambda) primarily through pricing and performance advantages rather than acknowledging rivals' strengths as motivation for self-improvement [1]. The competitive framing is about capturing market share in the AI cloud market, reflecting a finite competitive orientation typical of high-growth startups.

investment_thesis: Nebius is positioned at the center of the AI infrastructure buildout, delivering 684% revenue growth in 2025 to $529.8M with core AI cloud revenue growing 830% YoY in Q4 [1]. The company has guided to $3-3.4B revenue in 2026 with EBITDA margins approaching 40%, implying a path to profitability as scale economies take hold. The NVIDIA strategic alliance provides preferential GPU access, and $9.4B in cash supports the $16-20B capex plan. Forward PEG of 0.63 suggests the market is not fully pricing in the hypergrowth trajectory, and 94% renewable/nuclear energy sourcing creates a sustainability advantage as data center power becomes a competitive differentiator [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case is capital intensity and execution risk: Nebius plans $16-20B in capex for 2026 against $530M of 2025 revenue, requiring significant external financing that will dilute shareholders or increase leverage from the already-elevated 132% D/E ratio [1]. Operating margins are -32% and free cash flow is -$6.1B, meaning the company is burning cash rapidly and profitability depends on achieving massive scale. Forward P/E of 643x reflects a market pricing in perfection with zero margin for error. Competition from hyperscalers with $100B+ capex budgets (Microsoft, Google, Amazon) and neocloud peers like CoreWeave creates pricing pressure and customer concentration risk. The company's short operating history post-Yandex divestiture means there is no proven track record of managing data center operations at the scale it aspires to reach.
key_catalysts: Revenue acceleration toward the $3-3.4B 2026 guidance as new data center capacity in Finland, France, and the US comes online, validating the hypergrowth trajectory
EBITDA margin inflection toward the guided 40% level as infrastructure utilization improves, demonstrating a path to sustainable profitability at scale
NVIDIA strategic alliance deepening with next-generation GPU access, positioning Nebius as a preferred neocloud partner for enterprise AI workloads
key_risks: Capital intensity of $16-20B planned 2026 capex against $530M 2025 revenue creates existential execution risk if AI infrastructure demand slows or financing markets tighten
Hyperscaler competition from AWS, Azure, and GCP with $100B+ annual capex budgets could compress neocloud margins and limit Nebius's addressable market to price-sensitive customers
Forward P/E of 643x and -$6.1B free cash flow leave no margin of error — any revenue miss or demand deceleration could trigger severe multiple compression
sources: Nebius Group Q4 and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release — SEC Form 8-K, February 2026; Q1 2026 Revenue Update
Nebius Group CEO Arkady Volozh, Leadership, and AI Strategy — LinkedIn, Simply Wall St, Wikipedia, 2024-2026
Nebius Group ESG and Sustainability — Smart Energy Decisions, S&P Global, 2025-2026
